source_title: VB-MAPP Verbal Behavior Milestones Assessment Protocol

milestones.milestone_name: Mands for 20 different missing items without prompts (except, e.g., What do you need?)
milestones.example: mands for paper when given a crayon

milestones.milestone_name: Mands for others to emit 5 different actions or missing actions needed to enjoy a desired activity
milestones.example: open to get outside, push when on a swing

milestones.milestone_name: Emits 5 different mands that contain 2 or more words (not including, I want)
milestones.example: Go fast. My turn. Pour juice.

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously emits 15 different mands
milestones.example: Let's play. Open. I want book.

milestones.milestone_name: Emits 10 new mands without specific training
milestones.example: spontaneously says Where kitty go? without formal mand training

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts 25 items when asked, What's that?
milestones.example: book, shoe, car, dog, hat

milestones.milestone_name: Generalizes tacts across 3 examples of 50 items, tested or from a list of known generalizations
milestones.example: tacts 3 different cars

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts 10 actions when asked, for example, What am I doing?
milestones.example: jumping, sleeping, eating

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts 50 two-component verb-noun or noun-verb combinations, tested or from a list of known two-component tacts
milestones.example: washing face, Joe swinging, baby sleeping

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts a total of 200 nouns and/or verbs (or other parts of speech), tested or from an accumulated list of known tacts
Selects the correct item from a messy array of 6, for 40 different objects or pictures
milestones.example: Find cat. Touch ball.

milestones.milestone_name: Generalizes listener discriminations (LDs) in a messy array of 8, for 3 different examples of 50 items
milestones.example: the child can find 3 examples of a train

milestones.milestone_name: Performs 10 specific motor actions on command
milestones.example: Show me clapping. Can you hop?

milestones.milestone_name: Follows 50 two-component noun-verb and/or verb-noun instructions
milestones.example: Show me the baby sleeping. Push the swing.

milestones.milestone_name: Selects the correct item in a book, picture scene, or natural environment when named, for 250 items (tested or from an accumulated list of known words)
Matches identical objects or pictures in a messy array of 6, for 25 items
Sorts similar colors and shapes for 10 different colors or shapes, given models
Matches identical objects or pictures in a messy array of 8 containing 3 similar stimuli, for 25 items
Matches non-identical objects or pictures in a messy array of 8 containing 2 similar stimuli, for 25 items
Matches non-identical objects (3D) to pictures (2D) and/or vice versa, in a messy array of 10 containing 3 similar stimuli, for 25 items
Searches for a missing or corresponding toy or part of a set for 5 items or sets
milestones.example: a puzzle piece, a ball for a drop-in toy, a bottle for a baby doll

milestones.milestone_name: Independently demonstrates the use of toys or objects according to their function for 5 items
milestones.example: placing a train on a track, pulling a wagon, holding a telephone to the ear

milestones.milestone_name: Plays with everyday items in creative ways 2 times
milestones.example: uses a bowl as a drum or a box as an imaginary car

milestones.milestone_name: Independently engages in play on structures and playground equipment for a total of 5 minutes
milestones.example: going down a slide, swinging

milestones.milestone_name: Assembles toys that have multiple parts for 5 different sets of materials
milestones.example: Mr. Potato Head, Little People sets, Cooties bugs, Kid K'Nex

milestones.milestone_name: Initiates a physical interaction with a peer 2 times
milestones.example: a push in a wagon, hand holding, Ring Around the Rosy

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously mands to peers 5 times
milestones.example: My turn. Push me. Look! Come on.

milestones.milestone_name: Engages in sustained social play with peers for 3 minutes without adult prompts or reinforcement
milestones.example: cooperatively setting up a play set, water play

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously responds to the mands from peers 5 times
milestones.example: Pull me in the wagon. I want the train.

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously mands to peers to participate in games, social play, etc., 2 times
milestones.example: Come on you guys. Let's dig a hole.

milestones.milestone_name: Imitates 10 actions that require selecting a specific object from an array
milestones.example: selects a drumstick from an array also containing a horn and a bell, and imitates an adult's drumming

milestones.milestone_name: Imitates 20 different fine motor actions when prompted, Do this
milestones.example: wiggling fingers, pinching, making a fist, making a butterfly

milestones.milestone_name: Imitates 10 different three-component sequences of actions when prompted, Do this
milestones.example: clapping, jumping, touching toes; pick up a doll, place her in a crib, and rock the crib

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously imitates 5 functional skills in the natural environment
milestones.example: eating with a spoon, putting on a coat, removing shoes

milestones.milestone_name: Imitates (or attempts to with approximations) any novel motor action modeled by an adult with and without objects
milestones.example: a generalized imitative repertoire

milestones.milestone_name: Scores at least 50 on the EESA subtest (at least 20 from Group 2)
Scores at least 60 on the EESA subtest
Scores at least 70 on the EESA subtest
Scores at least 80 on the EESA subtest
Scores at least 90 on the EESA subtest (at least 10 from Groups 4 and 5)

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects an animal or object from an array of 3, for 5 different sounds made by the corresponding items
milestones.example: Meow says a... Toot, toot goes the...

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects 5 different foods or drinks when each is presented in an array of 5 (along with 4 non-food or non-drink items) and asked the verbal fill-ins You eat... and You drink...

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects the correct item from an array of 8, for 25 different LRFFC fill-in statements of any type
milestones.example: You sit on a...

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects the correct item from an array of 10 (or from a book), for 25 different verb-noun LRFFC what, which, or who questions
milestones.example: What do you ride? Which one barks? Who can hop?

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously tacts the item on 50% of the LRFFC trials
milestones.example: says dog given the verbal statement, Find an animal, and a visual array containing a picture of a dog

milestones.milestone_name: Completes 10 different fill-in-the-blank phrases of any type
milestones.example: song fill-ins, social games and fun fill-ins, animal or object sounds

milestones.milestone_name: Provides first name when asked, What is your name?
Completes 25 different fill-in-the-blank phrases (not including songs)
milestones.example: You eat... You sleep in a... Shoes and...

milestones.milestone_name: Answers 25 different what questions
milestones.example: What do you like to eat?

milestones.milestone_name: Answers 25 different who or where questions
milestones.example: Who's your friend? Where is your pillow?

milestones.milestone_name: Sits at a group snack or lunch table without negative behavior for 3 minutes
Puts away personal items, lines up, or comes to a table with only 1 verbal prompt
Transitions between classroom activities with no more than 1 gestural or verbal prompt
Sits in a small group of 3 or more children for 5 minutes without disruptive behavior or attempting to leave the group
Sits in a small group of 3 or more children for 10 minutes, attends to the teacher or material for 50% of the period, and responds to 5 of a teacher's SDs
The child's articulation of 10 tacts can be understood by familiar adults who cannot see the item tacted
Has a total listener vocabulary of 100 words
milestones.example: Touch nose. Jump. Find keys.

milestones.milestone_name: Emits 10 different 2-word utterances per day of any type except echoic
milestones.example: mand, tact

milestones.milestone_name: Emits functional prosody (i.e., rhythm, stress, intonation) on 5 occasions in one day
milestones.example: puts emphasis or stress on certain words such as, It's MINE!

milestones.milestone_name: Has a total speaker vocabulary size of 300 words (all verbal operants, except echoic)
